repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest catalogue entries are pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded, pid_rdd_flow_manipulation, and pid_multivariate_interval_regression, but none state a heap-specific bunching contrast-polytope frontier

published_prior_art.cite: ManskiTamer2002IntervalData and BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011ConvexMoment give general interval/random-set support-function machinery; this is a heap-specific bunching specialization with an aliasing certificate

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue proposal gives an equal-grid rank-zero recoverability screen for heaped bunching mass

published_prior_art.cite: BarrecaLindoWaddell2016HeapingRD, BoschDekkerStrohmaier2020Window, Dong2015RoundingRD, and Song2024GeneralBunching do not give this heap-nullspace rank threshold

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The rank-zero criterion is over the full heap nullspace, while Conjecture 1's point-ID condition is over feasible differences; boundary or degenerate P^H can make rank(R)=1 but leave no feasible aliasing direction.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Qualify Conjecture 2 as a design-uniform or interior-feasible criterion, or replace rank(R)=0 with a rank/recession condition on the feasible contrast cone A_G(q0)-A_G(q0).
Add a non-boundary worked instance where p_h is strictly positive, A_G has a nontrivial fiber, and the rank screen and feasible contrast-polytope endpoints agree exactly.

Sharpen Conjecture 1 against the generic interval/random-set literature by stating the heap-specific computational gain: input size, LP dimension, or explicit signed-fiber certificate not supplied by the general BMM support-function theorem.

recommended_next_step: Patch Conjecture 2 so the phase threshold matches the feasible-direction criterion, then resubmit without changing the core kernel.
